---
title: "Sig GWAS Bonferroni SNPs Checker"
author: "Elizabeth Campbell"
date: "2025-12-08"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
################################################################################
# IDENTIFY BONFERRONI-SIGNIFICANT SNPs IN ANCOVA RESULTS
################################################################################

library(dplyr)

# Paths
GWAS_DIR <- "C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2/Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations/GWAS"
ANCOVA_FILE <- "C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2/Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations/ANCOVA/ancova_full_results.csv"

# Bonferroni threshold
bonferroni_threshold <- 2.81e-6

cat("================================================================================\n")
cat("IDENTIFYING BONFERRONI-SIGNIFICANT SNPs\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# Get all GWAS files
gwas_files <- list.files(GWAS_DIR, pattern = "gwas_four_models_bio.*\\.csv", full.names = TRUE)

# Extract Bonferroni-significant SNPs from Q+K model
bonferroni_snps <- data.frame()

for (file in gwas_files) {
  bio_num <- gsub(".*bio(\\d+)\\.csv", "\\1", basename(file))
  bio_var <- paste0("wc2.1_30s_bio_", bio_num)
  
  gwas_data <- read.csv(file)
  
  # Filter to Q+K model and Bonferroni threshold
  bonf_sig <- gwas_data %>%
    filter(model == "Q+K" & p_value < bonferroni_threshold) %>%
    dplyr::select(marker, p_value) %>%
    mutate(bio_var = bio_var)
  
  bonferroni_snps <- rbind(bonferroni_snps, bonf_sig)
}

# Remove duplicates
bonferroni_snps <- bonferroni_snps %>%
  distinct(marker, bio_var, .keep_all = TRUE)

cat(sprintf("Bonferroni-significant associations (p < 2.81×10⁻⁶): %d\n", nrow(bonferroni_snps)))
cat(sprintf("Unique Bonferroni-significant SNPs: %d\n\n", length(unique(bonferroni_snps$marker))))

cat("Bonferroni-significant SNPs:\n")
bonf_summary <- bonferroni_snps %>%
  group_by(marker) %>%
  summarize(
    n_traits = n(),
    traits = paste(gsub("wc2.1_30s_bio_", "bio", bio_var), collapse = ", "),
    min_p = min(p_value)
  ) %>%
  arrange(min_p)
print(bonf_summary)

# Load ANCOVA results
ancova_results <- read.csv(ANCOVA_FILE)

cat("\n================================================================================\n")
cat("BONFERRONI SNPs IN ANCOVA RESULTS\n")
cat("================================================================================\n\n")

# Get unique Bonferroni SNPs
bonf_snp_list <- unique(bonferroni_snps$marker)

# Check which are in ANCOVA
ancova_snps <- unique(ancova_results$marker)

bonf_in_ancova <- bonf_snp_list[bonf_snp_list %in% ancova_snps]

cat(sprintf("Bonferroni SNPs tested in ANCOVA: %d of %d\n\n", 
            length(bonf_in_ancova), length(bonf_snp_list)))

if (length(bonf_in_ancova) > 0) {
  cat("These Bonferroni SNPs were tested:\n")
  for (snp in bonf_in_ancova) {
    # Check if it has significant interaction in ANCOVA
    snp_results <- ancova_results %>% filter(marker == snp)
    n_tests <- nrow(snp_results)
    n_sig_int <- sum(snp_results$significant_interaction, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    cat(sprintf("  • %s: %d test(s), %d significant interaction(s)\n", 
                snp, n_tests, n_sig_int))
  }
  
  # Summary
  cat("\n================================================================================\n")
  cat("SUMMARY: BONFERRONI SNPs WITH INTERACTIONS\n")
  cat("================================================================================\n\n")
  
  bonf_with_interactions <- 0
  for (snp in bonf_in_ancova) {
    snp_results <- ancova_results %>% filter(marker == snp)
    if (any(snp_results$significant_interaction, na.rm = TRUE)) {
      bonf_with_interactions <- bonf_with_interactions + 1
    }
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("Bonferroni SNPs with at least one significant interaction: %d of %d\n", 
              bonf_with_interactions, length(bonf_in_ancova)))
  cat(sprintf("Percentage: %.1f%%\n\n", 100 * bonf_with_interactions / length(bonf_in_ancova)))
  
  if (bonf_with_interactions == 9 && length(bonf_in_ancova) == 10) {
    cat("✓ This matches the claim: '9 of 10 genome-wide significant SNPs'\n")
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("⚠️ Claim should be: '%d of %d genome-wide significant SNPs'\n", 
                bonf_with_interactions, length(bonf_in_ancova)))
  }
}

cat("\n")
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
